narrative:
Unai Simón is Spain's starting goalkeeper, providing a secure presence between the posts. His overall rating of 7.27/10 places him among the top 8% of goalkeepers at this tournament. This rating is largely influenced by his strong club form over the last two seasons, accounting for 90% of his current score.

Simón has played 450 minutes at this World Cup, consistently performing his primary role. He averages 1.2 saves per 90 minutes, demonstrating his shot-stopping capabilities for Spain. His 0.4 clearances per 90 minutes indicate a focused approach to defensive responsibilities rather than expansive distribution. Furthermore, his expected assists per 90 minutes at this World Cup is 0.0.

Simón serves as a reliable anchor for the Spanish national team, with his rating range of 6.89–7.66 reflecting his steady performances. Scouts and fans should observe his continued ability to make crucial saves. His composure and decision-making will be key aspects to watch in Spain's remaining matches.
